generated_oml:
// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance MotorActuatorSystem : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motors (PMSMs) with integrated cycloidal gear transmissions for precise robotic joint motion control, enabling high-precision positioning and torque conversion for industrial robot applications"
]

instance GearTransmissionSystem : DTDFVocab:ActuatorComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision cycloidal gearboxes converting motor torque into controlled linear/rotational motion with specified actuation ranges and torque limitations for industrial robot joints"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Directly mounted motor encoder on permanent magnet synchronous motor providing real-time angular velocity measurements for robotic joint control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorCurrentData, AngularVelocityData
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Secondary encoder located behind gear transmission (cycloidal gear) providing spatial distribution-specific position measurements for precise control"
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionData
]

// C4: Data Transmission Components
instance MotorCurrentData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Processed input current data from robot motors via low-pass filtering to remove noise, transmitted via MQTT protocol"
]

instance AngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
    base:desc "Real-time angular velocity measurements of the motor, transmitted via MQTT protocol"
]

instance PositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
    base:desc "Position measurements of the first axis from secondary encoder, transmitted via MQTT protocol"
]

// C5: Data Components
instance FilteredCurrentData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Low-pass filtered current data traces from motor sensors for noise reduction"
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorCurrentData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LowPassFilter
]

instance AngularVelocityTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Real-time angular velocity traces from motor encoder for dynamic control calculations"
    DTDFVocab:fromData AngularVelocityData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkSimulation
]

instance PositionTrace : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Position traces from secondary encoder for joint control and trajectory planning"
    DTDFVocab:fromData PositionData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PositionControlModel
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking of friction characteristics (internal and external) for predictive maintenance and fault prevention in industrial robot systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionInsight
]

instance DynamicOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Dynamic optimization of machining parameters (cutting parameters) for process improvement and CO₂ reduction"
    DTDFVocab:provides ParameterOptimizationAction
]

instance FrictionPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive modeling of friction behavior under varying operational conditions for proactive maintenance"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionBehaviorInsight
]

instance ControlCompensationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time compensation of internal joint friction in control systems for improved positioning accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionCompensationAction
]

instance OperationalVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Centralized visualization of operational metrics (friction data, process parameters) for decision support"
    DTDFVocab:provides ProcessMetricsInsight
]

// C7: Models/Data Processing Enablers
instance MQTTProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT publish-subscribe protocol for hierarchical data routing using JSON-formatted messages via Mosquitto broker"
]

instance LowPassFilter : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Digital low-pass filter implementation for noise reduction in current sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionMonitoringService
]

instance SimulinkSimulation : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink environment for dynamic simulation of friction models, control loops, and process optimization"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionModel, PositionControlModel
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Processing logic for traceability of data traces to computational origins (preprocessing, simulation models)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FilteredCurrentData, AngularVelocityTrace, PositionTrace
]

instance FrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based friction modeling using Simulink FMUs for internal joint friction compensation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ControlCompensationService
]

instance PositionControlModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic positioning control model integrating real-time data traces for precision machining"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PositionTrace
]

instance DataCalculationModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning model for nonlinear relationship capture (e.g., friction compensation) using aggregated data traces"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FrictionPredictionService
]

// C8: Insights and Actions
instance FrictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Contextualized friction characteristic data traces aggregated from physical system measurements for predictive maintenance"
]

instance ParameterOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of machining parameters (cutting speeds, feeds) based on simulation-optimized values for process improvement"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance FrictionBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted friction behavior patterns under varying operational conditions enabling proactive maintenance planning"
]

instance FrictionCompensationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Real-time parameter adjustments to friction models for improved positioning accuracy in control systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ProcessMetricsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated operational metrics (friction data, environmental factors) visualized for decision-making support"
]

instance TraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Explicit associations linking all data traces to their computational origins (processing components, simulation models) ensuring model integrity"
]

// C9: System Environment
instance IndustrialRobotEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains MotorActuatorSystem, GearTransmissionSystem,
        MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, FrictionModel, PositionControlModel
]

instance ManufacturingWorkcell : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Precision machining environment integrating industrial robot system with machining tools and workpieces for dynamic adaptation"
]

instance ControlSystemArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Real-time control system architecture integrating Simulink-based simulations with physical robot interfaces"
]

instance DataTraceabilityEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains DataProcessingPipeline, SimulinkSimulation, FrictionModel,
        PositionControlModel, DataCalculationModel
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system employs **real-time synchronization** between physical and digital components, leveraging **live data streams** for continuous model updates. Synchronization occurs via **MQTT protocol** for efficient, low-latency communication, ensuring minimal delay in data propagation. The system integrates **SysML structural models** (for system architecture) and **Simulink behavioral models** (for physics-based friction modeling) to compute dynamic adjustments. While exact synchronization frequencies are not specified, the methodology supports **high-frequency data acquisition** (e.g., sensor inputs) to maintain precision in friction modeling. The temporal granularity is optimized for **real-time control applications**, where latency must be kept below critical thresholds for robotic motion accuracy. The pipeline ensures **autonomous, closed-loop refinement** of models (e.g., neural networks or physics-based white-box models) as new operational data (e.g., wear, temperature, payload) is ingested, enabling **continuous adaptation** of the Digital Twin’s predictive capabilities.
"
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system is structured using **SysML and Simulink models** for describing the robot’s structure and friction behavior, integrated via a **pipeline workflow** that processes live data and simulation models. The architecture does not explicitly mention an orchestration framework or resource management beyond the **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as a container for the digital shadow’s data and models. The system’s workflow is automated, with the AAS serving as a central repository for traceability and interoperability, but no formal orchestration patterns (e.g., FMI, AAS Executor) are referenced in the documents."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
    **Lifecycle Phases of a Digital Twin System (DT) in Manufacturing:**
    Based on the provided documents, the lifecycle of a Digital Twin system is structured around **concretization and conformance relations** from reference models, particularly in the context of **Model-Based Systems Engineering (MBSE)** and **Digital Shadow Reference Models**. The lifecycle can be conceptualized as follows:

    1. **Reference Model Definition (Conceptual Phase)**
       - **Representation Type:** Defined via **UML Class Diagrams (CDs)** and **architecture description languages (ADLs)** such as **ACME, AADL, MontiArc, or SysML IBDs**, capturing foundational concepts and relationships for the domain (e.g., manufacturing).
       - **Transition Management:** Transition occurs from abstract reference concepts to concrete realizations through **conformance relations**, ensuring alignment with domain-specific requirements (e.g., traceability via data traces and processing components).
       - **Lifecycle Governance:** Reference models are governed by **conformance validation** (e.g., mapping incarnations to reference concepts) to ensure adherence to domain standards (e.g., as described in [41]).

    2. **Concretization (Implementation Phase)**
       - **Representation Type:** Concrete models are realized using **heterogeneous modeling tools** (e.g., **SysML, MontiArc Automaton, Simulink, or code in C++/Java**) to describe components, interfaces, and behavior at different abstraction levels (e.g., **requirements → functional architecture → logical architecture → product/technical layer**).
       - **Transition Management:** Transitions are managed via **methodologies like RFLP (Requirements, Functions, Logics, Physics), OOSEM, SPES, or SMArDT**, ensuring modularity and interoperability between layers (e.g., [18], [20], [22]).
       - **Lifecycle Governance:** Governed by **model-driven engineering (MDE) practices**, where conformance relations enforce consistency between reference and concrete models (e.g., [41]).

    3. **Operational Phase (Digital Shadow/Digital Twin Runtime)**
       - **Representation Type:** Operates as a **Digital Shadow** (passive data aggregation) or **Digital Twin** (active processing), leveraging **data traces, processing components, and simulation models** (e.g., **MathWorks Simulink, DEVS discrete-event simulation tools**) to compute abstractions (e.g., [29], [30]).
       - **Transition Management:** Transitions between phases are facilitated by **purpose-driven design** (data-driven vs. requirement-driven) and **processing logic encoded in models** (e.g., [38]). Traceability is maintained via **associations originating from data traces** (e.g., [16]).
       - **Lifecycle Governance:** Governed by **active components** (e.g., [33]) and **processing workflows** (e.g., [38]), ensuring real-time data processing and autonomous decision-making in DTs.

    **Key Enablers:**
    - **Modeling Methodologies:** RFLP, OOSEM, SPES, SMArDT (for architecture description).
    - **Tools:** SysML, MontiArc, Simulink, AADL, MathWorks Simulink, DEVS.
    - **Reference Models:** UML Class Diagrams, ADLs (ACME, AADL, MontiArc), conformance relations (e.g., [41]).
    - **Lifecycle Governance:** Traceability via data traces, processing components, and simulation models.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin’s **fidelity and validity** are ensured through:
1. **Conformance relations**: Models adhere to a **Digital Shadow Reference Model**, ensuring consistency with domain-specific concepts and their relationships.
2. **Traceability**: Data traces and model transformations are linked back to their generating components, enabling verification of correctness and integrity.
3. **Model validation**: The system integrates **physics-based (white-box) and data-driven (black-box) models** to capture complex behaviors (e.g., friction phenomena), with validation methods ensuring accuracy under dynamic conditions (e.g., wear, temperature, payload changes).
4. **Error detection/correction**: Continuous data streams allow real-time updates to models (e.g., retraining neural networks or adjusting parameters) to compensate for deviations, ensuring adaptability and reliability.
5. **Quality assurance**: Traceability and conformance relations guarantee that all models and data traces are validated against the reference model, maintaining high fidelity and validity.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin technical connection** relies on the **MQTT protocol** for real-time data exchange, leveraging its lightweight, publish-subscribe architecture. The network topology is **hierarchical**, with MQTT topics organized in a structured manner to facilitate targeted data dissemination. While the documents do not specify explicit bandwidth or latency requirements, the system prioritizes **reliability** through message persistence mechanisms inherent in MQTT. Security is implicitly addressed via **read/write permissions** assigned to specific users/topics, though no explicit mention of TLS encryption is provided.
 "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
  The **Digital Twin hosting deployment** is implemented as a **cloud-based solution**, leveraging the **ThingsBoard IoT platform** for data storage and visualization. The system integrates **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** as the core data model, enabling standardized interoperability with Industry 4.0 assets. Computational requirements are met via **edge processing components** (preprocessing, MQTT broker) and cloud-based visualization dashboards. The deployment model supports **real-time scalability** through dynamic topic subscriptions, though no explicit redundancy or failover mechanisms are detailed. The infrastructure ensures **high availability** by maintaining continuous data flow from physical sensors to the digital shadow.
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system integrates with **MQTT** for streaming sensor data from the physical robot to the digital counterpart. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** is used as a standardized framework for interoperability, enabling seamless data exchange with other systems in the automation pyramid. The integration is purpose-driven, focusing on **real-time monitoring** and **friction modeling** for robot control, but no explicit mention of ThingsBoard, FMI, or enterprise system dependencies (e.g., production planning) is provided in the documents."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system adheres to **industry-standard modeling frameworks** and **conformance relations**:

1. **Industry Standards & Specifications**:
   - **Architecture Description Languages (ADLs)**: **AADL**, **MontiArc**, and **SysML** (e.g., SysML IBDs) for component/behavior modeling.
   - **Methodologies**: **RFLP**, **OOSEM**, **SPES**, and **SMArDT** for structured lifecycle phases.
   - **Reference Models**: UML class diagrams with **conformance relations** (e.g., mapping reference classes to concrete incarnations) to define domain concepts and their relationships.

2. **Compliance Frameworks**:
   - **Traceability Standards**: Enforced via **processing components** (data calculation/simulation models) to ensure alignment between physical and digital traces.
   - **No explicit certification requirements** or interoperability standards (e.g., ThingsBoard, CNCS digital shadows) are mentioned; compliance is achieved through **model-driven conformance** to reference models.
"
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system employs **MQTT protocol** (as referenced in [52]) for data communication, leveraging **Mosquitto** (MQTT broker) and **Thingsboard** (IoT platform, version 4.0.0, [53]) for streamed data exchange, though no explicit mention of secure messaging protocols (e.g., TLS/SSL encryption) is provided in the documents.

For physical safety, the system integrates a **safety cell** (implied by the context of manufacturing and robotics, e.g., [44] for MABI robotic arms) to ensure operator protection during operations. The **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** framework ([46], [47], [50]) is utilized to structure digital twin assets, with potential alignment to **IEC 62443** standards for cybersecurity (though this is not explicitly confirmed in the documents).

The **RAMI 4.0 reference architecture** ([48], [49]) is applied to define layers for cyber-physical integration, including **Physical Layer**, **Perception Layer**, **Network Layer**, and **Application Layer**, ensuring safety-critical system considerations through standardized interfaces and fail-safe mechanisms. The **Processing component** in the Digital Shadow Reference Model ([51], [55]) explicitly supports traceability and fault localization (e.g., [60]), though no explicit mention of threat mitigation strategies or access control mechanisms is documented."
]
